denial_letter_references.title: Internal claims and appeals — diagnosis/treatment codes (29 CFR § 2590.715-2719)
denial_letter_references.relevance: Calibrated P2 letter surface: Claim identification (DOS, provider, amount).

denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This notice concerns the prior authorization request for the requested service submitted on your behalf. Cigna has completed its review and has determined that the request cannot be approved.

The requested service was furnished by a provider who does not participate in your plan's network. Under your plan, coverage of the requested service requires that care be obtained from an in-network provider, or that a gap exception be approved in advance when no participating provider is reasonably available. Our records indicate that in-network alternatives were available and that no gap exception was approved. The requested service is therefore not covered.

This determination was made under the 2020 Cigna Medical Coverage Policy governing out-of-network imaging services, which sets the conditions under which such services may be covered.

This determination relates only to the network and authorization status described above. It does not represent a judgment about any other aspect of the request.

You may request a copy of your claim file and the criteria used in this determination at no charge. You have the right to appeal this determination within 180 days of the date of this notice by submitting a written request together with any additional information. A peer-to-peer discussion with a Cigna medical director may be arranged on request. If your appeal is unsuccessful, you may request an external review by an independent review organization.

Sincerely,
Cigna Coverage Review Department
clinical_context: The patient is a 64-year-old woman under surveillance for a known infrarenal abdominal aortic aneurysm (I71.4) measured at 4.8 cm during the first quarter of 2026, up from 4.5 cm on a scan six months earlier, a growth rate of roughly 0.6 cm per year that places her near the 5.0-5.5 cm threshold at which surgical or endovascular repair is considered. Her vascular surgeon ordered a surveillance abdominal ultrasound to confirm the current diameter and growth velocity before deciding on repair timing. The clinical record documents that the only accredited vascular laboratory able to perform the study within the clinically appropriate surveillance window was outside the plan network, the in-network facilities offered no appointment within that window, and the surgeon requested a gap exception on that basis. The record further notes that Cigna's current network-access policy, updated since 2020, grants a gap exception when no participating provider can furnish the study within the plan's access-time standard, and that delaying surveillance of an enlarging aneurysm risks missing the window for timely elective repair.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: Cell matches field-for-field; denial reason squarely OON.

scenario_realism.reasoning: Frustratingly realistic OON imaging denial with genuine exploitable gaps (stale policy, boilerplate, valid gap-exception basis).

insurer_voice.reasoning: Correctly Cigna-branded, no wrong-insurer tells, but generic Cigna Medical Coverage Policy rather than the signature MCG fingerprint (intended for the case-blind algo_boilerplate flaw).
insurer_voice.improvement: Cite MCG / Cigna Medical Necessity Guidelines for unmistakable house style.

denial_logic.reasoning: Authentically shoddy OON logic; vague on determinative facts; real policy.

clinical_realism.reasoning: AAA growth velocity, repair threshold, surveillance ultrasound modality all correct.

diagnosis_treatment_match.reasoning: Abdominal ultrasound is standard-of-care AAA surveillance.

diversity_delta.reasoning: AAA-surveillance-via-ultrasound unique vs all imaging neighbours.

diversity_statistical.reasoning: Max trigram-Jaccard: corpus=0.145 (nearest=case_195_cigna_mednec), neighbours=0.0. Thresholds: warn>=0.35, fail>=0.5.

safety_redactor.reasoning: No banned topics; AAA surveillance not end-of-life; Cigna commercial.

contradiction_hunter.reasoning: Core facts stable; 'in-network available' is the appealable falsehood, not a contradiction.

llm_tell_detector.reasoning: Formal boilerplate and dense clinical recitation; no conspicuous LLM tells.

overall_tone.reasoning: Cold procedural denial voice; neutral clinical narrative.

financial_auditor.reasoning: Silent on cost liability; standard practice.

legal_auditor.reasoning: 180-day window, external IRO review, claim-file rights, no hallucinated statutes; ERISA never named (realistic) and reliance on a stale policy.

demographic_validator.reasoning: 64F AAA epidemiologically reasonable; consistent female pronouns.

scope_guard.reasoning: Cigna PA, self-funded employer commercial (in-scope), adult 64, standard payer appeal language.

date_sanity.reasoning: No internal date contradiction; Q1-2026 timeline coherent and 180-day window correct, but letter is dateless and the cited 2020 policy is intentionally stale (the superseded flaw).

citation_traceability.reasoning: Correct Cigna brand, no fabricated code, but the named '2020' policy year is slightly specific (intentional, required to render the superseded-guideline flaw).
citation_traceability.improvement: Drop the year to stay fully umbrella-vague.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Stale 2020 policy applied and 'in-network available' premise rebutted by record; strong appeal.
exploitable_weaknesses: Denial relies on the outdated 2020 policy while the record cites Cigna's current updated network-access policy that grants a gap exception when no in-network provider meets the access-time standard

Denial's factual premise that 'in-network alternatives were available' is directly contradicted by the record showing no in-network facility could perform the study within the surveillance window, satisfying the denial's own gap-exception criterion
strong_defenses: Procedural disclosures are complete and correct: 180-day appeal window, free claim file/criteria access, peer-to-peer option, and external review rights are all stated
